title: agrostis coarctata subsp. hyperborea
intro: Agrostis coarctata subsp. hyperborea is a subspecies of grass that is native to the Arctic tundra, characterized by its short stature and narrow leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Agrostis coarctata subsp. hyperborea include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potential applications in treating infections and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
